principles.rule: Ship fewer pages if needed, but every page must answer a sharper question or provide a more tested angle than generic AI content.
Every article brief must force at least 3 non-generic insights from tasting notes, side-by-side tests, workflow logs, or service observations.
No template page should launch unless it can show real curation, useful comparison logic, and a meaningful related-article set.

principles.principle: Human verification where the cup or glass matters
principles.rule: Recipes, tasting notes, gear performance claims, historical assertions, and safety guidance require firsthand testing or expert review before publish.
Use a small number of durable clusters and reusable templates instead of exploding taxonomy depth too early.

architecture.pillarTitle: Coffee Brewing Fundamentals for Home Brewers
architecture.rationale: Entry cluster for foundational trust and broad internal linking into every coffee subtopic.
A small site can win by being precise on specific failure modes that bigger sites summarize poorly.

architecture.pillarTitle: Pour-Over Recipes and Brew Recipes by Device
architecture.rationale: Recipe pages are rankable when they are specific about bean style, water behavior, and failure points.
subclusters: V60 recipes by roast and goal
small batch vs large batch recipes
recipe adjustments for water and grinder differences

architecture.pillarTitle: Milk Drinks and Cafe-Style Coffee at Home
architecture.rationale: Strong beginner demand and monetization overlap with steam tools, pitchers, thermometers, and milk alternatives.

architecture.pillarTitle: Coffee Beans, Roast Levels, and Flavor Expectations
architecture.rationale: Useful bean content can outrank shallow affiliate lists when it teaches selection and expectation-setting.

architecture.pillarTitle: Water, Grinders, Extraction, and Ratios Explained
architecture.rationale: This cluster differentiates the site with practical process thinking instead of faux science or overclaimed precision.

architecture.pillarTitle: Original Cocktail Concepts for the Home Bar
architecture.rationale: Originality is the moat. These pages should focus on concept design, balance decisions, and deliberate flavor architecture.

architecture.pillarTitle: Spirit-Forward Cocktails and Stirred Drink Techniques
architecture.rationale: Competitive space, but winnable with precise dilution logic, ingredient substitutions, and home-bar realism.
subclusters: martini, Martinez, and aromatic stirred drinks
bar tools and ice for stirred drinks

architecture.rationale: Lower competition than classics-heavy queries and easy to make genuinely useful with carbonation and service detail.

architecture.pillarTitle: Dessert Cocktails That Still Drink Like Cocktails
architecture.rationale: Underserved angle: drinks that finish dessert-like without becoming syrupy or juvenile.
Seasonal and occasion pages are strong for long-tail discovery when they feel curated rather than mass-generated.

architecture.pillarTitle: Cocktail Components: Syrups, Infusions, Foams, and Garnishes
architecture.rationale: Component pages build authority and unlock many natural internal links into recipe pages.

architecture.pillarTitle: Flavor Pairing Guides for Coffee and Cocktails
architecture.rationale: This is a defensible angle if based on real sensory logic, not invented tasting poetry.
subclusters: cocktail spirit and flavor profile pairings

informational: Component technique pages for syrups, infusions, milk texture, and water setup
commercial: Milk pitchers, scales, kettles, and thermometers
Bar spoons, mixing glasses, strainers, ice molds, and carbonation tools
Bean selection pages by brew method or flavor goal
programmatic: Coffee bean by roast level and brew method
Ingredient hub pages for coffee and cocktail components
Flavor pair hubs only when enough article depth exists

internalLinkingRules.logic: Link from ratio, grind, and extraction explainers into specific espresso symptom pages using problem-first anchors.
Every fundamentals article should surface at least one recipe page and one troubleshooting page for practical next steps.
Use bean selection pages to route readers into recipes or troubleshooting advice tailored to roast level and flavor goal.
These should act as methodology hubs that contextualize why recipes or fixes work, not isolated science posts.
Link milk-drink pages back to espresso base quality pages so readers fix the shot before blaming the milk.
Original recipes should link to pairing pages that explain why a combination works and what to substitute.
Component pages should be linked only when they materially improve the featured drink and not as random tangents.
Use season and occasion pages as discovery hubs for lighter drinks with clear service contexts.

internalLinkingRules.target: Coffee cluster and flavor pairing guides
internalLinkingRules.logic: Cross-link coffee dessert cocktails into coffee flavor-note and after-dinner content where sensory overlap exists.
Programmatic pages must mainly link down into tested articles and up into pillar pages, never loop thin pages to each other.

programmaticPatterns.guardrails: Require at least 6 relevant articles or curated bean examples.
Only launch with clear sensory expectations and brew-method links.
Needs editorial criteria beyond listicle sorting.
Useful only with decision guidance, not product stuffing.
Must explain what changes with skill, not just restate basics.
Needs real recipe differences.
Requires tested recipes.
Only if batch size changes workflow materially.
High-value only with specific test-backed fixes.
Avoid thin pages for obscure machines.
Needs steaming behavior notes from testing.
Publish only where technique differs meaningfully.
Needs practical setup implications.
Commercial page must include bottleneck-based advice.
Should point to recipes and troubleshooting pages.
Use as strong hub pages, not thin archives.
Needs curation and substitution logic.
Only with distinctive service context.
Include weather, hosting, and ingredient logic.
Low priority unless serving context is strong.
Best used sparingly for enthusiast content.
Needs carbonation and ice guidance.
Should avoid repetitive body copy.
Only if profile changes recipe logic.
Must explain ratio and shelf life clearly.
Needs safety and over-extraction warnings.
Should link into relevant recipes and pairings.
Needs real pairing logic, not invented poetry.

programmaticPatterns.pattern: Cocktail flavor pair by spirit and note
programmaticPatterns.guardrails: Requires tested examples and substitution notes.
Only publish combinations with real editorial demand.
